derived_from.method: closed static inventory of exact v0.4.0 parser, process, workflow, and provider outcome families

terminal_mapping.inventory_family_set_closed_meaning: At the named source revision, every identified local source-terminal and provider-outcome family is represented by one of the 41 crosswalk rows. This is family-domain closure only; it does not assert that every current branch emits a discriminant or that provider outcomes are observable.
terminal_mapping.current_runtime_projection_ready_meaning: The v0.4 runtime does not emit the typed terminal projection, reason binding, provider acknowledgement, or status binding described here. No crosswalk row is implementation evidence.

GITHUB_STEP_OUTCOME.note: The workflow does not currently persist and bind every step outcome to a typed report; continue-on-error can make outcome and effective conclusion diverge.

GITHUB_JOB_RESULT.note: This is the closed needs/job-result domain used by the crosswalk. A skipped result is never semantic completion.

GITHUB_CHECK_CONCLUSION.note: A null or pending conclusion is an execution state, not another conclusion value. The current repository has no durable consumer acknowledgement binding this conclusion to the report and canonical status identity.

PROVIDER_OBSERVATION_STATE.note: This separates absence or loss of observation from the provider's terminal conclusion domain.

GITHUB_STEP_CONCLUSION.note: This is distinct from step outcome. Under continue-on-error, a failed outcome can have an effective successful conclusion.

binding_sentinel.meaning: The terminal family does not choose a generic reason. Runtime construction must bind the accepted reason already attached to the owning incomplete check.

normalization_rules: WHEN_NONCOMPLETED_FROM_TERMINAL and OWNING_NONCOMPLETION_REASON normalize to BOUND_TO_OWNING_CHECK_REASON.
Null reason targets normalize to an empty target_reason_codes list.
A row with more than one proposed terminal family requires its required_future_discriminant before runtime projection.
Provider-domain closure does not imply provider observation, caller reachability, output delivery, or status-publication readiness.

families.current_observable_projection: argparse writes help and raises SystemExit(0) before handler dispatch
families.required_future_discriminant: INVOCATION_HELP distinct from a semantic report exit 0

families.current_observable_projection: argparse writes usage to stderr and raises SystemExit(2)
families.required_future_discriminant: ARGPARSE_GRAMMAR_ERROR versus post-parse OPTION_COMBINATION_ERROR

families.current_observable_projection: aggregate has_errors selects exit 1 or 0; text or JSON is printed
families.required_future_discriminant: typed OverallOutcome and complete planned-check states; exit must agree with report

families.current_observable_projection: not representable; Report.emit selects only exit 0 or 1
families.required_future_discriminant: OverallOutcome.INDETERMINATE with explicit incomplete check and accepted reason_code

families.current_observable_projection: plain ERROR on stdout and sys.exit(1) before parser construction
families.required_future_discriminant: PYYAML_UNAVAILABLE | JSONSCHEMA_UNAVAILABLE, emitted on the bootstrap channel without pretending to be a policy finding

families.current_observable_projection: no top-level exception guard; traceback and interpreter exit commonly collapse to exit 1
families.required_future_discriminant: INTERNAL_FAILURE caught at a trusted boundary after preserving any already-completed check results

families.current_observable_projection: stdout print failure is uncaught; a consumer cannot distinguish absent, truncated or malformed output
families.required_future_discriminant: atomic typed-result handoff plus consumer validation of full document, catalog and plan binding

families.current_observable_projection: no signal handler; operating-system/interpreter convention applies
families.required_future_discriminant: bounded signal number supplied by the process supervisor; do not translate into a semantic reason

families.current_observable_projection: external action success/failure controls later-step reachability and job conclusion; no typed report
families.required_future_discriminant: provider action identity, step identity and provider conclusion acquired by a trusted adapter

families.current_observable_projection: external action failure produces job failure and skips dependent steps
families.required_future_discriminant: provider action conclusion distinct from dependency installation and validator execution

families.current_observable_projection: pip exit controls job failure; no typed bootstrap report
families.required_future_discriminant: DEPENDENCY_INSTALL_START_FAILED | DEPENDENCY_INSTALL_EXIT_NONZERO | DEPENDENCY_INSTALL_DEADLINE

families.current_observable_projection: validator exit 0/1 directly controls the step and job; no consumer validates report completeness
families.required_future_discriminant: typed report plus exit agreement; process exit without a valid report must never be inferred as policy FAIL

families.current_observable_projection: unittest process exit controls job failure; it is not part of the validator semantic report
families.required_future_discriminant: TEST_FAILURE versus test-runner/bootstrap/internal non-completion if this becomes a typed public check

families.current_observable_projection: container action success/failure is a separate GitHub job conclusion
families.required_future_discriminant: action completed with lint violations versus action image/pull/runtime/provider failure

families.current_observable_projection: workflow command error and shell exit 1
families.required_future_discriminant: missing repository, ref and SHA context represented as provider non-completion, not workflow-identity mismatch finding

families.current_observable_projection: inspection I/O failure and deterministic occupied-path finding both use SystemExit/nonzero
families.required_future_discriminant: DESTINATION_INSPECTION_IO_FAILED versus DESTINATION_OCCUPIED

families.current_observable_projection: external action failure marks its job failed and skips later steps; failure cause is only action/provider output
families.required_future_discriminant: ADOPTER_CHECKOUT_FAILED versus TRUSTED_PROFILE_CHECKOUT_FAILED, with job and action identity

families.current_observable_projection: external action failure marks job failed; no typed reason
families.required_future_discriminant: provider action outcome and exact planned job

families.current_observable_projection: pip exit controls job; no typed reason
families.required_future_discriminant: start, nonzero exit and deadline causes with exact planned job

families.current_observable_projection: deterministic policy findings, path/input acquisition errors, internal exceptions and GITHUB_OUTPUT write failures all collapse to step nonzero
families.required_future_discriminant: stable field/predicate identity before prose; separate acquire, parse, evaluate and publish-output phases

families.current_observable_projection: adoption_stage is passed through GITHUB_OUTPUT; missing output participates in a job if-expression without a typed missing-output state
families.required_future_discriminant: producer completed, output key present, output grammar valid, output bound to exact source report

families.current_observable_projection: step is skipped when version is unreleased; provider skip has no semantic state
families.required_future_discriminant: trusted plan records NOT_APPLICABLE before provider scheduling; skipped step itself is not proof

families.current_observable_projection: any git fetch failure is described as a nonexistent tag and exits 1; transport/auth/provider errors are indistinguishable
families.required_future_discriminant: TAG_ABSENT_ESTABLISHED | FETCH_TRANSPORT_FAILED | FETCH_AUTH_FAILED | FETCH_INTERNAL_FAILED | TAG_COMMIT_MISMATCH

families.current_observable_projection: validator exit directly becomes step/job conclusion; no typed result handoff validation
families.required_future_discriminant: typed report, exact plan/catalog binding and exit agreement

families.current_observable_projection: known query/response errors explicitly SystemExit(0); unexpected errors are ignored by continue-on-error and job can remain green
families.required_future_discriminant: CURRENT | TRAILING | QUERY_UNAVAILABLE | EMPTY_RESPONSE | INVALID_RESPONSE | INTERNAL_FAILURE; optional non-completion remains visible

families.current_observable_projection: DRAFT, failed/skipped structure dependency and missing adoption_stage output can all prevent execution; GitHub reports a skipped job without an AAP state
families.required_future_discriminant: trusted pre-scheduling state distinguishing stage N/A, failed dependency and missing/invalid output; blocked_by required for prerequisite failure

families.current_observable_projection: validator exit directly becomes step/job conclusion; no typed result handoff validation
families.required_future_discriminant: typed report, exact plan/catalog binding and exit agreement

families.current_observable_projection: all non-pull_request callers skip the job and emit no applicability/completion record
families.required_future_discriminant: ADR 0009 event classification must occur in a trusted always-reachable adapter before job gating; provider skip never substitutes for the state

families.current_observable_projection: Git diff failure, nonempty Git diagnostics, file I/O, malformed record handling and size limit failures become raw step failure
families.required_future_discriminant: GIT_START_FAILED | GIT_EXIT_NONZERO | GIT_DIAGNOSTIC_UNTRUSTED | INPUT_LIMIT | INPUT_IO | NORMALIZATION_INTERNAL

families.current_observable_projection: printf stages PR prose; process/I/O failure becomes raw step failure and later steps are skipped
families.required_future_discriminant: authoritative empty body versus failure to materialize provider body bytes

families.current_observable_projection: deterministic conditions, missing revisions, Git start/exit/stderr failures, 60-second evidence deadline, resource limits, malformed Git output, file I/O and uncaught internal errors collapse to raw exit 1
families.required_future_discriminant: separate acquired-condition findings from REVISION_UNAVAILABLE and EVIDENCE_START_FAILED | EVIDENCE_EXIT_NONZERO | EVIDENCE_DEADLINE | EVIDENCE_LIMIT | EVIDENCE_MALFORMED_OUTPUT | EVIDENCE_IO | INTERNAL_FAILURE

families.current_observable_projection: validator exit directly becomes drift job conclusion; no typed result handoff validation
families.required_future_discriminant: typed report, exact plan/catalog/event binding and exit agreement

families.current_observable_projection: default success() semantics skip later steps after a failure; individual planned checks do not receive explicit completion states
families.required_future_discriminant: provider step graph bound to canonical plan; blocked_by points to an existing failed or noncompleted prerequisite

families.current_observable_projection: failed staleness step does not fail the job and no later adapter reads steps outcome/conclusion
families.required_future_discriminant: capture both provider step outcome and conclusion, plus a typed optional check state

families.current_observable_projection: provider cancellation can stop all remaining steps; no in-workflow finalizer or trusted report is guaranteed
families.required_future_discriminant: trusted provider-side observation outside the cancelled job; an always() step alone is insufficient

families.current_observable_projection: invalid workflow definition, unsupported call contract, provider policy rejection or dispatch failure can prevent every job from being created; repository code emits no report
families.required_future_discriminant: provider-side invocation record distinguishing definition rejection, call-input rejection, policy rejection and provider dispatch failure

families.current_observable_projection: the reusable workflow cannot observe a caller call-job hidden behind false if/needs or absent on a required activity; a skipped caller job may be reported successful without invoking this workflow
families.required_future_discriminant: accepted static caller provenance plus provider invocation identity for every required activity; absence must invalidate complete GateCoverage

families.current_observable_projection: a job may remain queued, fail to acquire a runner, or lose its runner before output; no repository finalizer is guaranteed
families.required_future_discriminant: provider-side job lifecycle observation outside the affected runner, including queued/start/last-heartbeat/conclusion facts

families.current_observable_projection: neither workflow declares timeout-minutes; provider maximum or external cancellation can end work without a typed report
families.required_future_discriminant: accepted per-job deadline plus trusted provider-side timed_out conclusion distinct from cancellation

families.current_observable_projection: GitHub derives check/status conclusions from jobs; no explicit publisher or durable consumer acknowledgement binds the status identity to a typed report
families.required_future_discriminant: provider acknowledgement containing non-substitutable status identity, producer revision, report digest, plan identity and final conclusion; publisher cannot self-attest delivery

families.current_observable_projection: missing, malformed or stale reusable output has no independent terminal record and can alter downstream reachability
families.required_future_discriminant: output presence, grammar, producer step outcome, exact source report digest and consumer acknowledgement

rules: GITHUB_STEP_OUTCOME and GITHUB_STEP_CONCLUSION are observed as distinct axes.
A continue-on-error failure may preserve FAILURE outcome while producing SUCCESS conclusion; neither axis may be inferred from the other.
A step conclusion contributes to but does not alone determine GITHUB_JOB_RESULT because guards, cancellation, skipped prerequisites, and other steps remain observable inputs.
GITHUB_JOB_RESULT contributes to but does not alone prove GITHUB_CHECK_CONCLUSION or status publication.
PROVIDER_OBSERVATION_STATE is required after check conclusion and distinguishes absent, pending, unacknowledged, and unavailable observations.
provider_propagation_contract.family_reference_rule: Each provider-backed family inherits every downstream axis named by this contract in addition to its immediate provider_domain_refs.

steps.name: Check out this workflow's profile source (trusted)
Verify release pin matches its tag
Check out this workflow's profile source (trusted)
Verify upstream pin matches this workflow
Check out this workflow's profile source (trusted)
Write PR description to a file
Materialize the base tree and compute the assurance diff
Route impact and screen policy against the component map
